page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs and 170 GB of system memory. The processing power is delivered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor featuring 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. This instance is equipped with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, providing a total of 80 GB of VRAM. It does not include local NVMe storage and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall consistently within the middle 50% average. Conversely, the server excels in LLM inference benchmarks, achieving top-tier status in the top 10% for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models, such as processing 498.53 tokens per second on a 70B model.

Given the high-density hardware profile, this server represents a specialized resource allocation where cost efficiency is realized through accelerated parallel processing rather than general-purpose CPU compute. The lack of local storage requires external block storage integration, which is a key architectural trade-off. This configuration is highly optimized for deep learning, large language model inference, data science pipelines, and GPU-accelerated parallel computing workloads, while being less suitable for CPU-bound single-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for parallel computing and machine learning workloads. It features 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 physical cores, 170 GB of system memory, and two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs delivering 80 GB of total VRAM. The instance does not include local storage. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits weak single-core CPU performance but delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. However, it achieves top-tier performance in large language model inference, making it highly efficient for processing complex AI models. This hardware profile is best suited for deep learning, LLM inference, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks, representing a specialized trade-off that prioritizes massive parallel GPU throughput over raw CPU speed.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 170 GB RAM, and 2 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80 GB VRAM, built for top-tier LLM inference and deep learning.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g features 24 vCPUs, 170 GB RAM, and 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform pairing dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs with dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput parallel computing and LLM inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with 2 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing 80 GB of total VRAM
Features 24 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture with 170 GB RAM
Delivers top-tier benchmark results for small, medium, and large LLM inference
No local storage included, requiring external storage attachment
Best suited for deep learning, AI model execution, and parallel data processing
